response.justification: While Aisha demonstrates strong software craftsmanship, polyglot breadth, and some developer tooling exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI IDE work, MCP frameworks), the role demands deep Java/Kotlin/JVM expertise and hands-on Spring ecosystem experience — neither of which is substantively present in this resume. Java appears only peripherally (robotic arms, mobile orchestration tooling at Altom), Kotlin is entirely absent, and there is no mention of Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate. The candidate's primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, which is orthogonal to the JVM-centric enterprise IDE focus required here.
strengths: Prior exposure to IDE internals and developer tooling (NI LabWindows/CVI C IDE, MCP code generators, test frameworks)
Demonstrated software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and clean architecture across multiple roles
Broad polyglot background and experience with large-scale, high-throughput systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
concerns: No substantive Java or Kotlin experience; JVM depth is absent — the listed Java usage is minor and non-JVM-ecosystem-focused
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java framework in the entire employment history
Core expertise is in TypeScript/Node.js/DLang web and backend stacks, which are misaligned with JVM-based enterprise IDE development
